renamed-repo links, drop stale assessment artifacts (#2058)

CONTRIBUTING.md still pointed at the old `affaan-m/everything-claude-code`
repo URL in the Quick Start fork instructions and in the Issues link at
the bottom. Both relied on GitHub's silent rename-redirect, but the
literal `cd everything-claude-code` after `gh repo fork` would land in
the wrong directory now that the repo is `affaan-m/ECC`.

REPO-ASSESSMENT.md and EVALUATION.md were both 2026-03-21 personal
fork-audit artifacts written from one user's specific install. They
describe the project as a fork at `Infiniteyieldai/everything-claude-code`
v1.9.0 with 28 agents / 116 skills / 59 commands and pin the recommended
mode at "use as upstream tracker". None of that is true anymore (this
IS the upstream, v2.0.0-rc.1, currently 61 / 246 / 76). EVALUATION.md in
particular still references a defunct branch (`claude/evaluate-repo-comparison-ASZ9Y`)
and describes a "Current Setup" of zero installed components as if it
were universal, which it is not.

Neither file is referenced by anything else in the repo (`rg` confirmed)
and they actively mislead new contributors and visitors. Delete both.

A targeted line-by-line refresh of EVALUATION.md was considered but
rejected: bringing only the totals up to date (61/246/76) would leave
the rest of the document — v1.9.0 references, branch metadata, the
zero-component "Current Setup" — internally inconsistent (CodeRabbit
flagged this on the first revision of this PR). Wholesale removal is
the honest fix.

Translated copies (e.g. docs/pt-BR/README.md still has the 28/116/59
numbers) are intentionally left for a follow-up i18n PR to keep this
diff small." class="Link--secondary" href="/affaan-m/ECC/commit/d2dfca21a43a8a2faa7f759e8e86ffb82a3a806d">docs: fix renamed-repo links, drop stale assessment artifacts (</a><a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4519014917" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/2058" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/pull/2058/hovercard" href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/pull/2058" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#2058</a><a data-pjax="true" title="docs: fix renamed-repo links, drop stale assessment artifacts (#2058)

class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix: make plugin hooks run on Node 21+ and green the suite under modern Node (#2184)

ROOT CAUSE: hooks load plugin-hook-bootstrap.js via
`node -e "...; process.argv.splice(1,0,s); require(s)"`. On Node 21+,
require.main is `undefined` under --eval, so the `if (require.main === module)`
guard was false and main() never ran — every plugin hook silently no-op'd
(e.g. the MCP-health PreToolUse hook stopped blocking). CI (Node 18/20) hid
this; it only surfaces on Node 21+. Fix: also run main() when require.main is
undefined (the eval-bootstrap case), while staying dormant on real imports.

Also clears pre-existing main debt the full local suite enforces:
- catalog:sync — README/docs agent+skill counts drifted after recent merges
- tests/ci/supply-chain-watch-workflow: update checkout SHA to the merged v6.0.3 (#2183)
- markdownlint + check-unicode-safety --write across docs/skills

Suite: 2683/2683 green under Node v25; lint + unicode clean.

Co-authored-by: ECC Test <ecc@example.test>" class="Link--secondary" href="/affaan-m/ECC/commit/e755c5f72b9ce6011766b68d03cb02457668004b">fix: make plugin hooks run on Node 21+ and green the suite under mode…</a></div></div></td><td><div class="react-directory-commit-age"><relative-time tense="past" datetime="2026-06-07T08:05:28.000Z" title="Jun 7, 2026, 8:05 AM UTC">Jun 7, Add a repo-level supply-chain incident response playbook for npm/GitHub Actions package-registry incidents, anchored on the May 2026 TanStack compromise and prior Shai-Hulud-style npm incidents.

- add `docs/security/supply-chain-incident-response.md` with exposure checks, immediate response steps, workflow rules, publication rules, and escalation triggers
- link the playbook from `SECURITY.md`
- reject `pull_request_target` workflows that restore or save shared dependency caches
- add a regression test for the new `pull_request_target + actions/cache` guardrail

Validation:
- node tests/ci/validate-workflow-security.test.js (12 passed, 0 failed)
- node scripts/ci/validate-workflow-security.js (validated 7 workflow files)
- npx markdownlint-cli 'SECURITY.md' 'docs/security/supply-chain-incident-response.md'
- npx markdownlint-cli '**/*.md' --ignore node_modules
- git diff --check
- node tests/run-all.js (2377 passed, 0 failed)
- GitHub CI for #1848 green across Ubuntu, Windows, and macOS

language track with agents, skills, rules, and commands (#2024)

* feat(rules): add rules/react/ track

Five rule files mirroring per-language convention (coding-style,
hooks, patterns, security, testing). Each has `paths:` glob
frontmatter for auto-activation when editing matching files.

- coding-style.md: file extensions, naming, JSX, RSC boundary
- hooks.md: React hooks (NOT Claude Code hooks) — rules-of-hooks,
  dep arrays, cleanup, memoization, React 19 additions
- patterns.md: container/presentational split, state location
  decision tree, Suspense + error boundaries, forms, data fetching
- security.md: dangerouslySetInnerHTML, unsafe URL schemes,
  server-action validation, env-var leaks, CSP
- testing.md: RTL queries, userEvent, async, MSW, axe, anti-patterns

Each file extends typescript/* and common/* rules.

* feat(skills): add react-patterns, react-testing, react-performance

Three new skills under skills/ following the SKILL.md convention.

- react-patterns: React 18/19 idioms — hooks discipline, state
  location decision tree, server/client component boundary,
  Suspense + error boundaries, form actions (React 19), data
  fetching matrix, composition recipes, accessibility-first.
- react-testing: React Testing Library + Vitest/Jest, query
  priority order, userEvent, MSW network mocking, axe a11y
  assertions, RTL vs Playwright CT boundary, TDD workflow.
- react-performance: 70-rule performance ruleset adapted from
  Vercel Labs react-best-practices (MIT) across 8 priority
  categories — waterfalls, bundle size, server-side, client
  fetch, re-render, rendering, JS micro, advanced patterns.
  Includes Lighthouse / Web Vitals mapping and attribution to
  upstream.

Cross-links between the three skills and out to frontend-patterns,
accessibility, e2e-testing, tdd-workflow.

* feat(agents): add react-reviewer and react-build-resolver

Two new agents covering React-specific code review and build error
resolution, plus matching .kiro/ mirrors and a routing pointer
edit on typescript-reviewer.

- react-reviewer: slim React-only lanes (hooks rules,
  dangerouslySetInnerHTML, unsafe URL schemes, key prop, state
  mutation, derived-state-in-effect, server/client component
  boundary, accessibility, render performance, Server Action
  validation, env-var leaks). Explicitly delegates generic
  TypeScript/async/Node concerns to typescript-reviewer. Both
  agents should be invoked together on .tsx/.jsx PRs.
- react-build-resolver: React build/bundler/runtime hydration
  failures across Vite, webpack, Next.js, CRA, Parcel, esbuild,
  Bun, Rsbuild. Handles JSX/TSX compile errors, tsconfig fixes,
  Next.js App Router server/client boundary errors, hydration
  mismatches, duplicated React copies, Tailwind/PostCSS pipeline.
- .kiro/agents/react-reviewer.json + react-build-resolver.json:
  Kiro IDE format mirrors following the per-language precedent.
- typescript-reviewer: routing pointer added to its MEDIUM React
  block — defers to /react-review for React-specific concerns
  while keeping its block as fallback for repos that only invoke
  typescript-reviewer.

All agents carry the standard Prompt Defense Baseline stanza.

* feat(commands): add /react-review /react-build /react-test

Three new slash commands invoking the React agents.

- /react-review: invokes react-reviewer. Documents the routing
  rule with typescript-reviewer — both should run together on
  TSX/JSX PRs. Lists CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM rule categories and
  the automated checks (eslint with react-hooks + jsx-a11y,
  tsc --noEmit, npm audit).
- /react-build: invokes react-build-resolver. Documents bundler
  detection, common failure patterns, fix strategy, and stop
  conditions.
- /react-test: enforces TDD with React Testing Library + Vitest
  or Jest, behavior-focused queries, userEvent + MSW patterns,
  axe accessibility assertions, coverage targets.

Each command file has the required description: frontmatter and
follows the per-language command convention (cpp-test, go-test,
kotlin-test, etc.).

* chore: wire react track into manifests and stack mappings

- agent.yaml: add react-patterns, react-performance, react-testing
  to the skills array; add react-build, react-review, react-test to
  the commands array (alphabetically inserted to satisfy the
  ci/agent-yaml-surface sync test).
- config/project-stack-mappings.json: extend the `react` stack
  entry — add "react" to rules array (was ["common","typescript",
  "web"]); add react-patterns, react-performance, react-testing,
  accessibility to the skills array.
- docs/COMMAND-REGISTRY.json: bump totalCommands 75 -> 78; add
  three new entries (react-build, react-review, react-test) with
  primaryAgents / allAgents / skills wiring. react-review's
  allAgents includes typescript-reviewer to reflect the dual-agent
  routing convention.
- CLAUDE.md: add Skills-table row mapping *.tsx / *.jsx /
  components/** to react-patterns + react-testing skills and
  the /react-review, /react-build, /react-test commands.

* chore(catalog): sync counts to 62 agents / 78 commands / 235 skills

Auto-generated via `node scripts/ci/catalog.js --write --text`
after the react track additions:

- 2 new agents: react-reviewer, react-build-resolver (60 -> 62)
- 3 new commands: react-build, react-review, react-test (75 -> 78)
- 3 new skills: react-patterns, react-performance, react-testing
  (232 -> 235)

Files updated by the catalog sync:
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json description string
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json plugin description
- README.md quick-start summary, project tree, feature parity tables
- README.zh-CN.md quick-start summary
- AGENTS.md project structure summary
- docs/zh-CN/README.md parity table
- docs/zh-CN/AGENTS.md project structure summary

All counts now match the filesystem catalog (verified by
ci/catalog.test.js).

* feat(kiro): add react agent markdown companions to JSON entries

* feat(kiro): add react skills into manifests

* fix(ci): sync catalog counts, registry, and package files for react track

- .claude-plugin/{plugin,marketplace}.json: bump description counts to 62/235/78
- docs/COMMAND-REGISTRY.json: regenerate to include quality-gate and react commands
- package.json: add skills/react-{patterns,performance,testing}/ to files allowlist so npm-publish-surface aligns with install-modules manifest

* fix(react): address PR #2024 review feedback

Critical:
- Remove undefined/.claude/session-aliases.json containing __proto__ prototype-pollution
  fixture committed by accident in a7333c14

High:
- agents/react-build-resolver.md: replace brittle `test -o $(grep -l ...)` and
  `test -a -n $(grep ...)` detection with explicit `{ ... || grep -q ...; }` so
  bundler detection no longer breaks when grep returns empty
- agents/react-build-resolver.md: drop hardcoded `npm i react@^19 react-dom@^19`
  remediation; replace with version-agnostic pair-upgrade note that honors the
  project's installed major (17/18/19) — surgical fix principle
- commands/react-review.md: guard `tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json` with
  `[ -f tsconfig.json ] &&` so the review skips cleanly on JS-only projects

Medium:
- rules/react/security.md: correct the React-18-blocks-javascript-URL claim
  (React only warns in dev; production navigation is not blocked)
- rules/react/security.md: correct CRA env-var exposure row (CRA exposes
  REACT_APP_*, NODE_ENV, PUBLIC_URL — not 'all' variables)
- skills/react-testing/SKILL.md: instantiate QueryClient once outside the
  wrapper closure so React Query cache survives re-renders (flaky-test fix)
- skills/react-testing/SKILL.md: restore console.error spy with mockRestore()
  in a try/finally so the mock does not leak across tests
- commands/react-test.md: switch outer example-session fence to 4 backticks
  so the inner ```tsx/```bash blocks don't prematurely terminate it

* fix(kiro): mirror react-build-resolver react 19 conditional remediation

Discussion r3272907106 flagged the kiro json variant still carrying the hardcoded
'npm i react@^19 react-dom@^19' line that the .md companion already dropped.
Replace with the same conditional, version-agnostic guidance so both variants
stay in sync.

* fix(react): bump react-build example session fence to 4 backticks

Discussion r3272907144 flagged the same nested-fence issue in
commands/react-build.md that we fixed earlier in commands/react-test.md.
The outer triple-backtick text block was being prematurely terminated by
the inner bash/tsx fences inside the Example Session.

* fix(react): bump react-review example usage fence to 4 backticks

Discussion r3272907201 flagged the same nested-fence issue in
commands/react-review.md. The outer triple-backtick text block was
being prematurely terminated by the inner tsx/ts fences inside the
Example Usage transcript.

* fix(docs): clarify commands row as legacy shims in feature parity table

Discussion r3272912003: README comparison table said 'PASS: 78 commands'
while the install-section and quick-start prose use 'legacy command shims'.
Aligned the comparison-table cell to 'PASS: 78 commands (legacy shims)' so
the count word survives the catalog-validator regex while making the legacy
nature explicit.

Widened the catalog comparison-table commands regex to tolerate an optional
parenthetical after the count word, so both the existing 'X commands' and
the new 'X commands (legacy shims)' phrasings validate without breaking
older READMEs/translations.

* Update rules/react/security.md

Co-authored-by: cubic-dev-ai[bot] <191113872+cubic-dev-ai[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(react): guard tsc in react-build-resolver diagnostic commands

Discussion r3288910205: the agent prompt instructed an unconditional
'tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json', which adds noise (or hard-fails) on
JavaScript-only projects with no tsconfig.json or no installed TypeScript.

Replaced with 'test -f tsconfig.json && npx --yes tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json'
in both variants:
- agents/react-build-resolver.md
- .kiro/agents/react-build-resolver.json (prompt string mirrored)

Mirrors the same guard already applied to commands/react-review.md in de135f61.

* fix(react): pin tsc resolution to local install in build resolver

Discussion r3289054157: previous fix used 'npx --yes tsc' which auto-installs
the latest TypeScript from npm when none is local, producing version drift
and non-reproducible typecheck results across machines.

Switched to 'npx --no-install tsc' in both variants so the diagnostic uses
only the project's pinned TypeScript and fails fast if it isn't installed:
- agents/react-build-resolver.md
- .kiro/agents/react-build-resolver.json (prompt string mirrored)

* feat(counts): resolve counts for agents, skills...

* fix(ci): regen command registry for golang-testing entry

Removes stale kotlin-patterns entry to satisfy command-registry:check.

* fix: keep local Claude settings out of React track PR

data-discover="true">install.sh</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix: resolve MINGW64 double-path conversion in install.sh (#1015)

On Git Bash (MINGW64), the native Windows Node.js binary receives a
POSIX path from $SCRIPT_DIR (e.g. /g/projects/everything-claude-code)
which Git Bash auto-converts to G:\g\projects\... — doubling the
drive letter prefix and producing MODULE_NOT_FOUND errors.

Fix: use `cygpath -w` when available to explicitly convert the POSIX
path to a proper Windows path before passing it to Node, falling back
to the original path on non-MSYS/Cygwin environments." class="Link--secondary" href="/affaan-m/ECC/commit/a4d4b1d756f08f9127de3dc4b6b1a557c3449517">fix: resolve MINGW64 double-path conversion in install.sh (</a><a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4166452425" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/1015" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/pull/1015/hovercard" href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/pull/1015" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#1015</a><a data-pjax="true" title="fix: resolve MINGW64 double-path conversion in install.sh (#1015)

On Git Bash (MINGW64), the native Windows Node.js binary receives a
POSIX path from $SCRIPT_DIR (e.g. /g/projects/everything-claude-code)
which Git Bash auto-converts to G:\g\projects\... — doubling the
drive letter prefix and producing MODULE_NOT_FOUND errors.

Fix: use `cygpath -w` when available to explicitly convert the POSIX
path to a proper Windows path before passing it to Node, falling back
to the original path on non-MSYS/Cygwin environments." class="Link--secondary" href="/affaan-m/ECC/commit/a4d4b1d756f08f9127de3dc4b6b1a557c3449517">)</a></div></div></td><td><div install manifest packaging gaps (#2172)

- commands-core now ships scripts/harness-audit.js and scripts/skills-health.js:
  the module installs the whole commands/ dir, so /harness-audit and
  /skill-health were installed without their backing engines on
  manifest-driven installs (the original 1.10.0 failure mode)
- agentic-patterns now ships scripts/claw.js: the module installs the
  nanoclaw-repl skill, whose workflow operates scripts/claw.js
- package.json files array gains scripts/skills-health.js so the npm publish
  surface stays aligned with the module graph (claw.js and harness-audit.js
  were already listed)
- orchestration drops commands/multi-workflow.md and commands/sessions.md
  from its explicit paths: both are already shipped by commands-core, which
  is a declared dependency of the module, so the duplicate ownership produced
  two copy operations per destination in install-state. The two scripts/lib
  entries are kept because hooks-runtime is NOT a declared dependency and a
  standalone orchestration install still needs them" class="Link--secondary" href="/affaan-m/ECC/commit/b189e8ec9f6757c76b5816625d2491759b72e7a4">fix: close install manifest packaging gaps (</a><a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4600844689" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/2172" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/pull/2172/hovercard" href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/pull/2172" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#2172</a><a data-pjax="true" title="fix: close install manifest packaging gaps (#2172)

- package.json files array gains scripts/skills-health.js so the npm publish

<hr>
<p dir="auto"><strong>The harness-native operator system for agentic work. Built from real-world multi-harness engineering workflows.</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">Not just configs. A complete system: skills, instincts, memory optimization, continuous learning, security scanning, and research-first development. Production-ready agents, skills, hooks, rules, MCP configurations, and legacy command shims evolved over 10+ months of intensive daily use building real products.</p>
<p dir="auto">Works across <strong>Codex</strong>, <strong>Claude Code</strong>, <strong>Cursor</strong>, <strong>OpenCode</strong>, <strong>Gemini</strong>, <strong>Zed</strong>, <strong>GitHub Copilot</strong>, and other AI agent harnesses.</p>
<p dir="auto">ECC v2.0.0-rc.1 adds the public Hermes operator story on top of that reusable layer: start with the <a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/docs/HERMES-SETUP.md">Hermes setup guide</a>, then review the <a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/docs/releases/2.0.0-rc.1/release-notes.md">rc.1 release notes</a> and <a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/docs/architecture/cross-harness.md">cross-harness architecture</a>.</p>

<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Dashboard GUI</strong> — New Tkinter-based desktop application (<code>ecc_dashboard.py</code> or <code>npm run dashboard</code>) with dark/light theme toggle, font customization, and project logo in header and taskbar.</li>
<li><strong>Public surface synced to the live repo</strong> — metadata, catalog counts, plugin manifests, and install-facing docs now match the actual OSS surface: 64 agents, 255 skills, and 79 legacy command shims.</li>
<li><strong>Operator and outbound workflow expansion</strong> — <code>brand-voice</code>, <code>social-graph-ranker</code>, <code>connections-optimizer</code>, <code>customer-billing-ops</code>, <code>ecc-tools-cost-audit</code>, <code>google-workspace-ops</code>, <code>project-flow-ops</code>, and <code>workspace-surface-audit</code> round out the operator lane.</li>
<li><strong>Media and launch tooling</strong> — <code>manim-video</code>, <code>remotion-video-creation</code>, and upgraded social publishing surfaces make technical explainers and launch content part of the same system.</li>
<li><strong>Framework and product surface growth</strong> — <code>nestjs-patterns</code>, richer Codex/OpenCode install surfaces, and expanded cross-harness packaging keep the repo usable beyond Claude Code alone.</li>
<li><strong>Itô prediction-market skill pack</strong> — <code>ito-market-intelligence</code>, <code>ito-basket-compare</code>, <code>ito-trade-planner</code>, <code>ito-data-atlas-agent</code>, <code>prediction-market-oracle-research</code>, and <code>prediction-market-risk-review</code> add public, non-advisory market/basket workflows while keeping live Itô API access gated and separate from ECC Tools billing.</li>
<li><strong>Optimization skill pack</strong> — <code>parallel-execution-optimizer</code>, <code>benchmark-optimization-loop</code>, <code>data-throughput-accelerator</code>, <code>latency-critical-systems</code>, and <code>recursive-decision-ledger</code> turn repeated speed/recursion prompts into bounded benchmark, throughput, and decision-ledger workflows.</li>
<li><strong>ECC 2.0 alpha is in-tree</strong> — the Rust control-plane prototype in <code>ecc2/</code> now builds locally and exposes <code>dashboard</code>, <code>start</code>, <code>sessions</code>, <code>status</code>, <code>stop</code>, <code>resume</code>, and <code>daemon</code> commands. It is usable as an alpha, not yet a general release.</li>
<li><strong>Operator status snapshots</strong> — <code>ecc status --markdown --write status.md</code> turns the local state store into a portable handoff covering readiness, active sessions, skill-run health, install health, pending governance events, and linked work items from Linear/GitHub/handoffs. Use <code>ecc work-items upsert ...</code> for manual entries, <code>ecc work-items sync-github --repo owner/repo</code> for PR/issue queue state, and <code>ecc status --exit-code</code> to fail automation when readiness needs attention.</li>
<li><strong>Ecosystem hardening</strong> — AgentShield, ECC Tools cost controls, billing portal work, and website refreshes continue to ship around the core plugin instead of drifting into separate silos.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v1.9.0 — Selective Install & Language Expansion (Mar 2026)</h3><a id="user-content-v190--selective-install--language-expansion-mar-2026" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v1.9.0 — Selective Install & Language Expansion (Mar 2026)" href="#v190--selective-install--language-expansion-mar-2026"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

</ul>

<li><strong>Python/Django support</strong> — Django patterns, security, TDD, and verification skills</li>
<li><strong>Java Spring Boot skills</strong> — Patterns, security, TDD, and verification for Spring Boot</li>
<li><strong>Session management</strong> — <code>/sessions</code> command for session history</li>
<li><strong>Continuous learning v2</strong> — Instinct-based learning with confidence scoring, import/export, evolution</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">See the full changelog in <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/releases">Releases</a>.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Quick Start</h2><a id="user-content-quick-start" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Quick Start" href="#quick-start"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Get up and running in under 2 minutes:</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Pick one path only</h3><a id="user-content-pick-one-path-only" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Pick one path only" href="#pick-one-path-only"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Most Claude Code users should use exactly one install path:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Recommended default:</strong> install the Claude Code plugin, then copy only the rule folders you actually want.</li>
<li><strong>Use the manual installer only if</strong> you want finer-grained control, want to avoid the plugin path entirely, or your Claude Code build has trouble resolving the self-hosted marketplace entry.</li>
<li><strong>Do not stack install methods.</strong> The most common broken setup is: <code>/plugin install</code> first, then <code>install.sh --profile full</code> or <code>npx ecc-install --profile full</code> afterward.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">If you already layered multiple installs and things look duplicated, skip straight to <a href="#reset--uninstall-ecc">Reset / Uninstall ECC</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Low-context / no-hooks path</h3><a id="user-content-low-context--no-hooks-path" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Low-context / no-hooks path" href="#low-context--no-hooks-path"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Step 1: Install the Plugin (Recommended)</h3><a id="user-content-step-1-install-the-plugin-recommended" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Step 1: Install the Plugin (Recommended)" href="#step-1-install-the-plugin-recommended"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">NOTE: The plugin is convenient, but the OSS installer below is still the most reliable path if your Claude Code build has trouble resolving self-hosted marketplace entries.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Add marketplace</span>
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Install plugin</span>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Naming + Migration Note</h3><a id="user-content-naming--migration-note" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Naming + Migration Note" href="#naming--migration-note"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">ECC now has three public identifiers, and they are not interchangeable:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>GitHub source repo: <code>affaan-m/ECC</code></li>
<li>Claude marketplace/plugin identifier: <code>ecc@ecc</code></li>
<li>npm package: <code>ecc-universal</code></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">This is intentional. Anthropic marketplace/plugin installs are keyed by a canonical plugin identifier, so ECC uses <code>ecc@ecc</code> to keep tool names and slash-command namespaces short enough for strict Desktop/API validators. Older posts may still show the former long marketplace identifier; treat that as a legacy alias only. Separately, the npm package stayed on <code>ecc-universal</code>, so npm installs and marketplace installs intentionally use different names.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Step 2: Install Rules Only If You Need Them</h3><a id="user-content-step-2-install-rules-only-if-you-need-them" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Step 2: Install Rules Only If You Need Them" href="#step-2-install-rules-only-if-you-need-them"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">WARNING: <strong>Important:</strong> Claude Code plugins cannot distribute <code>rules</code> automatically.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you already installed ECC via <code>/plugin install</code>, <strong>do not run <code>./install.sh --profile full</code>, <code>.\install.ps1 --profile full</code>, or <code>npx ecc-install --profile full</code> afterward</strong>. The plugin already loads ECC skills, commands, and hooks. Running the full installer after a plugin install copies those same surfaces into your user directories and can create duplicate skills plus duplicate runtime behavior.</p>
<p dir="auto">For plugin installs, manually copy only the <code>rules/</code> directories you want under <code>~/.claude/rules/ecc/</code>. Start with <code>rules/common</code> plus one language or framework pack you actually use. Do not copy every rules directory unless you explicitly want all of that context in Claude.</p>
<p dir="auto">Use the full installer only when you are doing a fully manual ECC install instead of the plugin path.</p>
<p dir="auto">If your local Claude setup was wiped or reset, that does not mean you need to repurchase ECC. Start with <code>node scripts/ecc.js list-installed</code>, then run <code>node scripts/ecc.js doctor</code> and <code>node scripts/ecc.js repair</code> before reinstalling anything. That usually restores ECC-managed files without rebuilding your setup. If the problem is account or marketplace access for ECC Tools, handle billing/account recovery separately.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Clone the repo first</span>
git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC.git
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> ECC

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Install dependencies (pick your package manager)</span>
npm install        <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> or: pnpm install | yarn install | bun install</span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Plugin install path: copy only ECC rules into an ECC-owned namespace</span>
mkdir -p <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc
cp -R rules/common <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -R rules/typescript <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Fully manual ECC install path (use this instead of /plugin install)</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> ./install.sh --profile full</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

# Plugin install path: copy only ECC rules into an ECC-owned namespace

cp -R rules/common ~/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -R rules/typescript ~/.claude/rules/ecc/

# Fully manual ECC install path (use this instead of /plugin install)
# ./install.sh --profile full" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">For manual install instructions see the README in the <code>rules/</code> folder. When copying rules manually, copy the whole language directory (for example <code>rules/common</code> or <code>rules/golang</code>), not the files inside it, so relative references keep working and filenames do not collide.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Reset / Uninstall ECC</h3><a id="user-content-reset--uninstall-ecc" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Reset / Uninstall ECC" href="#reset--uninstall-ecc"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If ECC feels duplicated, intrusive, or broken, do not keep reinstalling it on top of itself.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Plugin path:</strong> remove the plugin from Claude Code, then delete the specific rule folders you manually copied under <code>~/.claude/rules/ecc/</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Manual installer / CLI path:</strong> from the repo root, preview removal first:</li>
</ul>

<p dir="auto">ECC only removes files recorded in its install-state. It will not delete unrelated files it did not install.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you stacked methods, clean up in this order:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Remove the Claude Code plugin install.</li>
<li>Run the ECC uninstall command from the repo root to remove install-state-managed files.</li>
<li>Delete any extra rule folders you copied manually and no longer want.</li>
<li>Reinstall once, using a single path.</li>
</ol>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Step 3: Start Using</h3><a id="user-content-step-3-start-using" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Step 3: Start Using" href="#step-3-start-using"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Multi-model commands require additional setup</h3><a id="user-content-multi-model-commands-require-additional-setup" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Multi-model commands require additional setup" href="#multi-model-commands-require-additional-setup"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">WARNING: <code>multi-*</code> commands are <strong>not</strong> covered by the base plugin/rules install above.</p>
<p dir="auto">To use <code>/multi-plan</code>, <code>/multi-execute</code>, <code>/multi-backend</code>, <code>/multi-frontend</code>, and <code>/multi-workflow</code>, you must also install the <code>ccg-workflow</code> runtime.</p>
<p dir="auto">Initialize it with <code>npx ccg-workflow</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">That runtime provides the external dependencies these commands expect, including:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>~/.claude/bin/codeagent-wrapper</code></li>
<li><code>~/.claude/.ccg/prompts/*</code></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Without <code>ccg-workflow</code>, these <code>multi-*</code> commands will not run correctly.</p>
</blockquote>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Cross-Platform Support</h2><a id="user-content-cross-platform-support" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Cross-Platform Support" href="#cross-platform-support"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This plugin now fully supports <strong>Windows, macOS, and Linux</strong>, alongside tight integration across major IDEs (Cursor, Zed, OpenCode, Antigravity) and CLI harnesses. All hooks and scripts have been rewritten in Node.js for maximum compatibility.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Package Manager Detection</h3><a id="user-content-package-manager-detection" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Package Manager Detection" href="#package-manager-detection"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The plugin automatically detects your preferred package manager (npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun) with the following priority:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>Environment variable</strong>: <code>CLAUDE_PACKAGE_MANAGER</code></li>
<li><strong>Project config</strong>: <code>.claude/package-manager.json</code></li>
<li><strong>package.json</strong>: <code>packageManager</code> field</li>
<li><strong>Lock file</strong>: Detection from package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, or bun.lockb</li>
<li><strong>Global config</strong>: <code>~/.claude/package-manager.json</code></li>
<li><strong>Fallback</strong>: First available package manager</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">To set your preferred package manager:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Via environment variable</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> CLAUDE_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pnpm

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Via global config</span>
node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --global pnpm

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Via project config</span>
node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --project bun

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Detect current setting</span>
node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --detect</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Via environment variable
export CLAUDE_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pnpm

# Via global config
node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --global pnpm

# Via project config
node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --project bun

# Detect current setting
node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --detect" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">Or use the <code>/setup-pm</code> command in Claude Code.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Hook Runtime Controls</h3><a id="user-content-hook-runtime-controls" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Hook Runtime Controls" href="#hook-runtime-controls"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Use runtime flags to tune strictness or disable specific hooks temporarily:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Hook strictness profile (default: standard)</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_HOOK_PROFILE=standard

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Comma-separated hook IDs to disable</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS=<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>pre:bash:tmux-reminder,post:edit:typecheck<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Cap SessionStart additional context (default: 8000 chars)</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_SESSION_START_MAX_CHARS=4000

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Disable SessionStart additional context entirely for low-context/local-model setups</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_SESSION_START_CONTEXT=off

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Session-tmp retention window in days (default: 30).</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Set to 0, off, false, disabled, never, or none to keep all sessions (disable pruning).</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_SESSION_RETENTION_DAYS=14

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Keep context/scope/loop warnings but suppress API-rate cost estimates</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_CONTEXT_MONITOR_COST_WARNINGS=off</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Hook strictness profile (default: standard)
export ECC_HOOK_PROFILE=standard

# Comma-separated hook IDs to disable
export ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS="pre:bash:tmux-reminder,post:edit:typecheck"

# Cap SessionStart additional context (default: 8000 chars)
export ECC_SESSION_START_MAX_CHARS=4000

# Disable SessionStart additional context entirely for low-context/local-model setups
export ECC_SESSION_START_CONTEXT=off

# Session-tmp retention window in days (default: 30).
# Set to 0, off, false, disabled, never, or none to keep all sessions (disable pruning).
export ECC_SESSION_RETENTION_DAYS=14

# Keep context/scope/loop warnings but suppress API-rate cost estimates
export ECC_CONTEXT_MONITOR_COST_WARNINGS=off" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Agent data home (multi-harness isolation)</h3><a id="user-content-agent-data-home-multi-harness-isolation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Agent data home (multi-harness isolation)" href="#agent-data-home-multi-harness-isolation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Memory persistence hooks (session summaries, learned skills, session aliases, metrics) store data under a single agent data root. By default that root is <code>~/.claude</code>. When you use ECC in both Claude Code and Cursor on the same machine, set a separate root for Cursor so the two environments do not overwrite each other's session files:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Cursor-only boundary (Claude Code keeps the default ~/.claude)</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME=<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span><span class="pl-smi">$HOME</span>/.cursor/ecc<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Cursor-only boundary (Claude Code keeps the default ~/.claude)
export ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME="$HOME/.cursor/ecc"" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Paths resolved under that root include:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>$ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME/session-data/</code> — session summaries</li>
<li><code>$ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME/skills/learned/</code> — learned skills from evaluate-session</li>
<li><code>$ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME/session-aliases.json</code> — session aliases</li>
<li><code>$ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME/metrics/</code> — cost and activity metrics</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">See <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/2065" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/issues/2065/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">affaan-m/ECC#2065</a>.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What's Inside</h2><a id="user-content-whats-inside" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What's Inside" href="#whats-inside"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This repo is a <strong>Claude Code plugin</strong> - install it directly or copy components manually.</p>

<p dir="auto">Two ways to generate Claude Code skills from your repository:</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Option A: Local Analysis (Built-in)</h4><a id="user-content-option-a-local-analysis-built-in" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Option A: Local Analysis (Built-in)" href="#option-a-local-analysis-built-in"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Use the <code>/skill-create</code> command for local analysis without external services:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>/skill-create                    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Analyze current repo</span>

<p dir="auto">Built at the Claude Code Hackathon (Cerebral Valley x Anthropic, Feb 2026). 1282 tests, 98% coverage, 102 static analysis rules.</p>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Scan your Claude Code configuration for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and injection risks.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Quick scan (no install needed)</span>
npx ecc-agentshield scan

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Auto-fix safe issues</span>
npx ecc-agentshield scan --fix

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Deep analysis with three Opus 4.6 agents</span>
npx ecc-agentshield scan --opus --stream

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Generate secure config from scratch</span>
npx ecc-agentshield init</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

# Auto-fix safe issues
npx ecc-agentshield scan --fix

# Deep analysis with three Opus 4.6 agents
npx ecc-agentshield scan --opus --stream

# Generate secure config from scratch
npx ecc-agentshield init" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto"><strong>What it scans:</strong> CLAUDE.md, settings.json, MCP configs, hooks, agent definitions, and skills across 5 categories — secrets detection (14 patterns), permission auditing, hook injection analysis, MCP server risk profiling, and agent config review.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>The <code>--opus</code> flag</strong> runs three Claude Opus 4.6 agents in a red-team/blue-team/auditor pipeline. The attacker finds exploit chains, the defender evaluates protections, and the auditor synthesizes both into a prioritized risk assessment. Adversarial reasoning, not just pattern matching.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Output formats:</strong> Terminal (color-graded A-F), JSON (CI pipelines), Markdown, HTML. Exit code 2 on critical findings for build gates.</p>
<p dir="auto">Use <code>/security-scan</code> in Claude Code to run it, or add to CI with the <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/agentshield">GitHub Action</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/agentshield">GitHub</a> | <a href="https://www.npmjs.com/package/ecc-agentshield" rel="nofollow">npm</a></p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Important: Hooks Auto-Loading Behavior</h3><a id="user-content-important-hooks-auto-loading-behavior" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Important: Hooks Auto-Loading Behavior" href="#important-hooks-auto-loading-behavior"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">WARNING: <strong>For Contributors:</strong> Do NOT add a <code>"hooks"</code> field to <code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code>. This is enforced by a regression test.</p>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Claude Code v2.1+ <strong>automatically loads</strong> <code>hooks/hooks.json</code> from any installed plugin by convention. Explicitly declaring it in <code>plugin.json</code> causes a duplicate detection error:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>Duplicate hooks file detected: ./hooks/hooks.json resolves to already-loaded file

<p dir="auto"><strong>History:</strong> This has caused repeated fix/revert cycles in this repo (<a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/29" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/issues/29/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#29</a>, <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/52" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/issues/52/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#52</a>, <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/103" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/issues/103/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#103</a>). The behavior changed between Claude Code versions, leading to confusion. We now have a regression test to prevent this from being reintroduced.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Installation</h2><a id="user-content-installation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Installation" href="#installation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Option 1: Install as Plugin (Recommended)</h3><a id="user-content-option-1-install-as-plugin-recommended" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Option 1: Install as Plugin (Recommended)" href="#option-1-install-as-plugin-recommended"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The easiest way to use this repo - install as a Claude Code plugin:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Add this repo as a marketplace</span>
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Install the plugin</span>
/plugin install ecc@ecc</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Add this repo as a marketplace
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC

# Install the plugin
/plugin install ecc@ecc" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Or add directly to your <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code>:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"extraKnownMarketplaces"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"ecc"</span>: {
      <span class="pl-ent">"source"</span>: {
        <span class="pl-ent">"source"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>github<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
        <span class="pl-ent">"repo"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>affaan-m/ECC<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
      }
    }
  },
  <span class="pl-ent">"enabledPlugins"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"ecc@ecc"</span>: <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
  }
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">This gives you instant access to all commands, agents, skills, and hooks.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Note:</strong> The Claude Code plugin system does not support distributing <code>rules</code> via plugins (<a href="https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference" rel="nofollow">upstream limitation</a>). You need to install rules manually:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Clone the repo first</span>
git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC.git
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> ECC

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Option A: User-level rules (applies to all projects)</span>
mkdir -p <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc
cp -r rules/common <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/typescript <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/   <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> pick your stack</span>
cp -r rules/python <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/golang <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/php <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Option B: Project-level rules (applies to current project only)</span>
mkdir -p .claude/rules/ecc
cp -r rules/common .claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/typescript .claude/rules/ecc/     <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> pick your stack</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Clone the repo first
git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC.git
cd ECC

# Option A: User-level rules (applies to all projects)
mkdir -p ~/.claude/rules/ecc
cp -r rules/common ~/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/typescript ~/.claude/rules/ecc/   # pick your stack
cp -r rules/python ~/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/golang ~/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/php ~/.claude/rules/ecc/

# Option B: Project-level rules (applies to current project only)
mkdir -p .claude/rules/ecc
cp -r rules/common .claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/typescript .claude/rules/ecc/     # pick your stack" tabindex="0" role="button">

<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Option 2: Manual Installation</h3><a id="user-content-option-2-manual-installation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Option 2: Manual Installation" href="#option-2-manual-installation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If you prefer manual control over what's installed:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Clone the repo</span>
git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC.git
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> ECC

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Copy agents to your Claude config</span>
cp agents/<span class="pl-k">*</span>.md <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/agents/

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Copy rules directories (common + language-specific)</span>
mkdir -p <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc
cp -r rules/common <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/typescript <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/   <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> pick your stack</span>
cp -r rules/python <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/golang <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/php <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/arkts <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Copy skills first (primary workflow surface)</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Recommended (new users): core/general skills only</span>
mkdir -p <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/skills
cp -r .agents/skills/<span class="pl-k">*</span> <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/skills/
cp -r skills/search-first <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/skills/
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Claude Code loads skills only from direct children of ~/.claude/skills.</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Do not nest manual installs under ~/.claude/skills/ecc/.</span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Optional: add niche/framework-specific skills only when needed</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> for s in django-patterns django-tdd laravel-patterns springboot-patterns quarkus-patterns; do</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> cp -r skills/$s ~/.claude/skills/</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> done</span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Optional: keep maintained slash-command compatibility during migration</span>
mkdir -p <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/commands
cp commands/<span class="pl-k">*</span>.md <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/commands/

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Retired shims live in legacy-command-shims/commands/.</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Copy individual files from there only if you still need old names such as /tdd.</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC.git
cd ECC

# Copy agents to your Claude config
cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/agents/

# Copy rules directories (common + language-specific)
mkdir -p ~/.claude/rules/ecc
cp -r rules/common ~/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/typescript ~/.claude/rules/ecc/   # pick your stack
cp -r rules/python ~/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/golang ~/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/php ~/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/arkts ~/.claude/rules/ecc/

# Copy skills first (primary workflow surface)
# Recommended (new users): core/general skills only
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills
cp -r .agents/skills/* ~/.claude/skills/
cp -r skills/search-first ~/.claude/skills/
# Claude Code loads skills only from direct children of ~/.claude/skills.
# Do not nest manual installs under ~/.claude/skills/ecc/.

# Optional: add niche/framework-specific skills only when needed
# for s in django-patterns django-tdd laravel-patterns springboot-patterns quarkus-patterns; do
# cp -r skills/$s ~/.claude/skills/
# done

# Optional: keep maintained slash-command compatibility during migration
mkdir -p ~/.claude/commands
cp commands/*.md ~/.claude/commands/

# Retired shims live in legacy-command-shims/commands/.
# Copy individual files from there only if you still need old names such as /tdd." tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Install hooks</h4><a id="user-content-install-hooks" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Install hooks" href="#install-hooks"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Do not copy the raw repo <code>hooks/hooks.json</code> into <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code> or <code>~/.claude/hooks/hooks.json</code>. That file is plugin/repo-oriented and is meant to be installed through the ECC installer or loaded as a plugin, so raw copying is not a supported manual install path.</p>
<p dir="auto">Use the installer to install only the Claude hook runtime so command paths are rewritten correctly:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> macOS / Linux</span>
bash ./install.sh --target claude --modules hooks-runtime</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

bash ./install.sh --target claude --modules hooks-runtime" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">That writes resolved hooks to <code>~/.claude/hooks/hooks.json</code> and leaves any existing <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code> untouched.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you installed ECC via <code>/plugin install</code>, do not copy those hooks into <code>settings.json</code>. Claude Code v2.1+ already auto-loads plugin <code>hooks/hooks.json</code>, and duplicating them in <code>settings.json</code> causes duplicate execution and cross-platform hook conflicts.</p>
<p dir="auto">Windows note: the Claude config directory is <code>%USERPROFILE%\\.claude</code>, not <code>~/claude</code>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Configure MCPs</h4><a id="user-content-configure-mcps" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Configure MCPs" href="#configure-mcps"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Claude plugin installs intentionally do not auto-enable ECC's bundled MCP server definitions. This avoids overlong plugin MCP tool names on strict third-party gateways while keeping manual MCP setup available.</p>
<p dir="auto">Use Claude Code's <code>/mcp</code> command or CLI-managed MCP setup for live Claude Code server changes. Use <code>/mcp</code> for Claude Code runtime disables; Claude Code persists those choices in <code>~/.claude.json</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">For repo-local MCP access, copy desired MCP server definitions from <code>mcp-configs/mcp-servers.json</code> into a project-scoped <code>.mcp.json</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you already run your own copies of ECC-bundled MCPs, set:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_DISABLED_MCPS=<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>github,context7,exa,playwright,sequential-thinking,memory<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="export ECC_DISABLED_MCPS="github,context7,exa,playwright,sequential-thinking,memory"" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

</svg>
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">

<p dir="auto">ECC-managed install and Codex sync flows will skip or remove those bundled servers instead of re-adding duplicates. <code>ECC_DISABLED_MCPS</code> is an ECC install/sync filter, not a live Claude Code toggle.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Important:</strong> Replace <code>YOUR_*_HERE</code> placeholders with your actual API keys.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Key Concepts</h2><a id="user-content-key-concepts" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Key Concepts" href="#key-concepts"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Agents</h3><a id="user-content-agents" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Agents" href="#agents"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Subagents handle delegated tasks with limited scope. Example:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-text-md notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-s">---</span>
<span class="pl-ent">name</span>: <span class="pl-s">code-reviewer</span>
<span class="pl-ent">description</span>: <span class="pl-s">Reviews code for quality, security, and maintainability</span>
<span class="pl-ent">tools</span>: <span class="pl-s">["Read", "Grep", "Glob", "Bash"]</span>
<span class="pl-ent">model</span>: <span class="pl-s">opus</span>
<span class="pl-s">---</span>

You are a senior code reviewer...</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="---
name: code-reviewer
description: Reviews code for quality, security, and maintainability
tools: ["Read", "Grep", "Glob", "Bash"]
model: opus
---

You are a senior code reviewer..." tabindex="0" role="button">
