<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Skills</h3><a id="user-content-skills" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Skills" href="#skills"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Skills are the primary workflow surface. They can be invoked directly, suggested automatically, and reused by agents. ECC still ships maintained <code>commands/</code> during migration, while retired short-name shims live under <code>legacy-command-shims/</code> for explicit opt-in only. New workflow development should land in <code>skills/</code> first.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-text-md notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-mh"># <span class="pl-en">TDD Workflow</span></span>

<span class="pl-s">1</span><span class="pl-v">.</span> Define interfaces first
<span class="pl-s">2</span><span class="pl-v">.</span> Write failing tests (RED)
<span class="pl-s">3</span><span class="pl-v">.</span> Implement minimal code (GREEN)
<span class="pl-s">4</span><span class="pl-v">.</span> Refactor (IMPROVE)
<span class="pl-s">5</span><span class="pl-v">.</span> Verify 80%+ coverage</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# TDD Workflow

1. Define interfaces first
2. Write failing tests (RED)
3. Implement minimal code (GREEN)
4. Refactor (IMPROVE)
5. Verify 80%+ coverage" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Hooks</h3><a id="user-content-hooks" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Hooks" href="#hooks"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Hooks fire on tool events. Example - warn about console.log:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"matcher"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>tool == <span class="pl-cce">\"</span>Edit<span class="pl-cce">\"</span> && tool_input.file_path matches <span class="pl-cce">\"\\\\</span>.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$<span class="pl-cce">\"</span><span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
  <span class="pl-ent">"hooks"</span>: [{
    <span class="pl-ent">"type"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>command<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
    <span class="pl-ent">"command"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>#!/bin/bash<span class="pl-cce">\n</span>grep -n 'console<span class="pl-cce">\\</span>.log' <span class="pl-cce">\"</span>$file_path<span class="pl-cce">\"</span> && echo '[Hook] Remove console.log' >&2<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
  }]
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="{
  "matcher": "tool == \"Edit\" && tool_input.file_path matches \"\\\\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$\"",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "#!/bin/bash\ngrep -n 'console\\.log' \"$file_path\" && echo '[Hook] Remove console.log' >&2"
  }]
}" tabindex="0" role="button">

</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Not sure where to start? Use this quick reference. Skills are the canonical workflow surface; maintained slash entries stay available for command-first workflows.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>I want to...</th>
<th>Use this surface</th>
<th>Agent used</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Plan a new feature</td>
<td><code>/ecc:plan "Add auth"</code></td>
<td>planner</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Design system architecture</td>
<td><code>/ecc:plan</code> + architect agent</td>
<td>architect</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Write code with tests first</td>
<td><code>tdd-workflow</code> skill</td>
<td>tdd-guide</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Review code I just wrote</td>
<td><code>/code-review</code></td>
<td>code-reviewer</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Fix a failing build</td>
<td><code>/build-fix</code></td>
<td>build-error-resolver</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Run end-to-end tests</td>
<td><code>e2e-testing</code> skill</td>
<td>e2e-runner</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Find security vulnerabilities</td>
<td><code>/security-scan</code></td>
<td>security-reviewer</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Remove dead code</td>
<td><code>/refactor-clean</code></td>
<td>refactor-cleaner</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Update documentation</td>
<td><code>/update-docs</code></td>
<td>doc-updater</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Review Go code</td>
<td><code>/go-review</code></td>
<td>go-reviewer</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Review Python code</td>
<td><code>/python-review</code></td>
<td>python-reviewer</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Review F# code</td>
<td><em>(invoke <code>fsharp-reviewer</code> directly)</em></td>
<td>fsharp-reviewer</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Review TypeScript/JavaScript code</td>
<td><em>(invoke <code>typescript-reviewer</code> directly)</em></td>
<td>typescript-reviewer</td>
</tr>

<details>
<summary><b>My hooks aren't working / I see "Duplicate hooks file" errors</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">This is the most common issue. <strong>Do NOT add a <code>"hooks"</code> field to <code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code>.</strong> Claude Code v2.1+ automatically loads <code>hooks/hooks.json</code> from installed plugins. Explicitly declaring it causes duplicate detection errors. See <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/29" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/issues/29/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#29</a>, <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/52" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/issues/52/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#52</a>, <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/issues/103" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/issues/103/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#103</a>.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Can I use ECC with Claude Code on a custom API endpoint or model gateway?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes. ECC does not hardcode Anthropic-hosted transport settings. It runs locally through Claude Code's normal CLI/plugin surface, so it works with:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Anthropic-hosted Claude Code</li>
<li>Official Claude Code gateway setups using <code>ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL</code> and <code>ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN</code></li>
<li>Compatible custom endpoints that speak the Anthropic API Claude Code expects</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Minimal example:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">export</span> ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://your-gateway.example.com
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=your-token
claude</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://your-gateway.example.com
export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=your-token
claude" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">If your gateway remaps model names, configure that in Claude Code rather than in ECC. ECC's hooks, skills, commands, and rules are model-provider agnostic once the <code>claude</code> CLI is already working.</p>
<p dir="auto">Official references:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/llm-gateway" rel="nofollow">Claude Code LLM gateway docs</a></li>
<li><a href="https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/model-config" rel="nofollow">Claude Code model configuration docs</a></li>
</ul>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>My context window is shrinking / Claude is running out of context</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Too many MCP servers eat your context. Each MCP tool description consumes tokens from your 200k window, potentially reducing it to ~70k. SessionStart context is capped at 8000 characters by default; lower it with <code>ECC_SESSION_START_MAX_CHARS=4000</code> or disable it with <code>ECC_SESSION_START_CONTEXT=off</code> for local-model or low-context setups.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Fix:</strong> Disable unused MCPs from Claude Code with <code>/mcp</code>. Claude Code writes those runtime choices to <code>~/.claude.json</code>; <code>.claude/settings.json</code> and <code>.claude/settings.local.json</code> are not reliable toggles for already-loaded MCP servers.</p>
<p dir="auto">Keep under 10 MCPs enabled and under 80 tools active.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Can I use only some components (e.g., just agents)?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes. Use Option 2 (manual installation) and copy only what you need:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Just agents</span>
cp agents/<span class="pl-k">*</span>.md <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/agents/

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Just rules</span>
mkdir -p <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/
cp -r rules/common <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/rules/ecc/</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/agents/

cp -r rules/common ~/.claude/rules/ecc/" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Each component is fully independent.</p>

<details>
<summary><b>Does this work with Cursor / OpenCode / Codex / Antigravity / GitHub Copilot?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes. ECC is cross-platform:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Cursor</strong>: Pre-translated configs in <code>.cursor/</code>. See <a href="#cursor-ide-support">Cursor IDE Support</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Gemini CLI</strong>: Experimental project-local support via <code>.gemini/GEMINI.md</code> and shared installer plumbing.</li>
<li><strong>OpenCode</strong>: Full plugin support in <code>.opencode/</code>. See <a href="#opencode-support">OpenCode Support</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Codex</strong>: First-class support for both macOS app and CLI, with adapter drift guards and SessionStart fallback. See PR <a href="https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/pull/257" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/affaan-m/ECC/pull/257/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#257</a>.</li>
<li><strong>GitHub Copilot (VS Code)</strong>: Instruction and prompt layer via <code>.github/copilot-instructions.md</code>, <code>.vscode/settings.json</code>, and <code>.github/prompts/</code>. See <a href="#github-copilot-support">GitHub Copilot Support</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Antigravity</strong>: Tightly integrated setup for workflows, skills, and flattened rules in <code>.agent/</code>. See <a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/docs/ANTIGRAVITY-GUIDE.md">Antigravity Guide</a>.</li>
<li><strong>JoyCode / CodeBuddy</strong>: Project-local selective install adapters for commands, agents, skills, and flattened rules. See <a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/docs/JOYCODE-GUIDE.md">JoyCode Adapter Guide</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Qwen CLI</strong>: Home-directory selective install adapter for commands, agents, skills, rules, and Qwen config. See <a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/docs/QWEN-GUIDE.md">Qwen CLI Adapter Guide</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Zed</strong>: Project-local selective install adapter for <code>.zed/settings.json</code>, flattened rules, commands, agents, and skills.</li>
<li><strong>Non-native harnesses</strong>: Manual fallback path for Grok and similar interfaces. See <a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/docs/MANUAL-ADAPTATION-GUIDE.md">Manual Adaptation Guide</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Claude Code</strong>: Native — this is the primary target.</li>
</ul>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>How do I contribute a new skill or agent?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">See <a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md">CONTRIBUTING.md</a>. The short version:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Fork the repo</li>
<li>Create your skill in <code>skills/your-skill-name/SKILL.md</code> (with YAML frontmatter)</li>
<li>Or create an agent in <code>agents/your-agent.md</code></li>
<li>Submit a PR with a clear description of what it does and when to use it</li>
</ol>
</details>

<p dir="auto"><strong>Contributions are welcome and encouraged.</strong></p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Cursor Loading Notes</h3><a id="user-content-cursor-loading-notes" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Cursor Loading Notes" href="#cursor-loading-notes"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">ECC does not install root <code>AGENTS.md</code> into <code>.cursor/</code>. Cursor treats nested <code>AGENTS.md</code> files as directory context, so copying ECC's repo identity into a host project would pollute that project.</p>
<p dir="auto">Cursor-native loading behavior can vary by Cursor build. ECC installs agents as <code>.cursor/agents/ecc-*.md</code>; if your Cursor build does not expose project agents, those files still work as explicit reference definitions instead of hidden global prompt context.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Memory and data isolation (Cursor + Claude Code)</h3><a id="user-content-memory-and-data-isolation-cursor--claude-code" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Memory and data isolation (Cursor + Claude Code)" href="#memory-and-data-isolation-cursor--claude-code"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">ECC memory hooks reuse the same <code>scripts/hooks/*.js</code> as Claude Code. For Cursor, ECC tries to keep memory <strong>out of <code>~/.claude</code> automatically</strong>:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>Cursor <code>sessionStart</code> hook</strong> (installed to <code>.cursor/hooks.json</code> on <code>--target cursor</code>) injects <code>ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME</code> for the whole composer session.</li>
<li><strong>Hook runtime default</strong> — when <code>CURSOR_VERSION</code> or <code>CURSOR_PROJECT_DIR</code> is present, hooks default to <code>~/.cursor/ecc</code> if the env var is unset.</li>
<li><strong>Project config</strong> — <code>.cursor/ecc-agent-data.json</code> documents and overrides the path (<code>agentDataHome</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Always-on rule</strong> — <code>.cursor/rules/ecc-agent-data-home.mdc</code> reminds the agent where memory lives.</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">You can still override explicitly:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">export</span> ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME=<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span><span class="pl-smi">$HOME</span>/.cursor/ecc<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="export ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME="$HOME/.cursor/ecc"" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">
    <path d="M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z"></path><path d="M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z"></path>

<p dir="auto">To <strong>share</strong> memory with Claude Code on purpose, set <code>ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME=~/.claude</code> in the shell or in <code>.cursor/ecc-agent-data.json</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">Continuous learning v2 instincts remain separate under <code>CLV2_HOMUNCULUS_DIR</code> (default <code>~/.local/share/ecc-homunculus</code>).</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Hook Architecture (DRY Adapter Pattern)</h3><a id="user-content-hook-architecture-dry-adapter-pattern" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Hook Architecture (DRY Adapter Pattern)" href="#hook-architecture-dry-adapter-pattern"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Cursor has <strong>more hook events than Claude Code</strong> (20 vs 8). The <code>.cursor/hooks/adapter.js</code> module transforms Cursor's stdin JSON to Claude Code's format, allowing existing <code>scripts/hooks/*.js</code> to be reused without duplication.</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>Cursor stdin JSON → adapter.js → transforms → scripts/hooks/*.js
                                              (shared with Claude Code)
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="Cursor stdin JSON → adapter.js → transforms → scripts/hooks/*.js
                                              (shared with Claude Code)" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Key hooks:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>beforeShellExecution</strong> — Blocks dev servers outside tmux (exit 2), git push review</li>
<li><strong>afterFileEdit</strong> — Auto-format + TypeScript check + console.log warning</li>
<li><strong>beforeSubmitPrompt</strong> — Detects secrets (sk-, ghp_, AKIA patterns) in prompts</li>
<li><strong>beforeTabFileRead</strong> — Blocks Tab from reading .env, .key, .pem files (exit 2)</li>
<li><strong>beforeMCPExecution / afterMCPExecution</strong> — MCP audit logging</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Rules Format</h3><a id="user-content-rules-format" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Rules Format" href="#rules-format"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Cursor rules use YAML frontmatter with <code>description</code>, <code>globs</code>, and <code>alwaysApply</code>:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>---
<span class="pl-ent">description</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>TypeScript coding style extending common rules<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
<span class="pl-ent">globs</span>: <span class="pl-s">["**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", "**/*.js", "**/*.jsx"]</span>
<span class="pl-ent">alwaysApply</span>: <span class="pl-c1">false</span>
---</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

description: "TypeScript coding style extending common rules"
globs: ["**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", "**/*.js", "**/*.jsx"]
alwaysApply: false
---" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Codex macOS App + CLI Support</h2><a id="user-content-codex-macos-app--cli-support" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Codex macOS App + CLI Support" href="#codex-macos-app--cli-support"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">ECC provides <strong>first-class Codex support</strong> for both the macOS app and CLI, with a reference configuration, Codex-specific AGENTS.md supplement, and shared skills.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Quick Start (Codex App + CLI)</h3><a id="user-content-quick-start-codex-app--cli" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Quick Start (Codex App + CLI)" href="#quick-start-codex-app--cli"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<p dir="auto">The sync script safely merges ECC MCP servers into your existing <code>~/.codex/config.toml</code> using an <strong>add-only</strong> strategy — it never removes or modifies your existing servers. Run with <code>--dry-run</code> to preview changes, or <code>--update-mcp</code> to force-refresh ECC servers to the latest recommended config.</p>
<p dir="auto">For Context7, ECC uses the canonical Codex section name <code>[mcp_servers.context7]</code> while still launching the <code>@upstash/context7-mcp</code> package. If you already have a legacy <code>[mcp_servers.context7-mcp]</code> entry, <code>--update-mcp</code> migrates it to the canonical section name.</p>
<p dir="auto">Codex macOS app:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Open this repository as your workspace.</li>
<li>The root <code>AGENTS.md</code> is auto-detected.</li>
<li><code>.codex/config.toml</code> and <code>.codex/agents/*.toml</code> work best when kept project-local.</li>
<li>The reference <code>.codex/config.toml</code> intentionally does not pin <code>model</code> or <code>model_provider</code>, so Codex uses its own current default unless you override it.</li>
<li>Optional: copy <code>.codex/config.toml</code> to <code>~/.codex/config.toml</code> for global defaults; keep the multi-agent role files project-local unless you also copy <code>.codex/agents/</code>.</li>
</ul>

<p dir="auto">Codex does <strong>not yet provide Claude-style hook execution parity</strong>. ECC enforcement there is instruction-based via <code>AGENTS.md</code>, optional <code>model_instructions_file</code> overrides, and sandbox/approval settings.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Multi-Agent Support</h3><a id="user-content-multi-agent-support" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Multi-Agent Support" href="#multi-agent-support"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Current Codex builds support stable multi-agent workflows.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Enable <code>features.multi_agent = true</code> in <code>.codex/config.toml</code></li>
<li>Define roles under <code>[agents.<name>]</code></li>
<li>Point each role at a file under <code>.codex/agents/</code></li>
<li>Use <code>/agent</code> in the CLI to inspect or steer child agents</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">ECC ships three sample role configs:</p>

<thead>
<tr>
<th>Role</th>
<th>Purpose</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>explorer</code></td>
<td>Read-only codebase evidence gathering before edits</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>reviewer</code></td>
<td>Correctness, security, and missing-test review</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>docs_researcher</code></td>
<td>Documentation and API verification before release/docs changes</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

<p dir="auto">The adapter writes ECC-managed files under <code>.zed/</code> and keeps BYOK/OpenRouter credentials out of the repo. Configure Zed account or API keys through Zed's own settings UI or your local user settings.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">OpenCode Support</h2><a id="user-content-opencode-support" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: OpenCode Support" href="#opencode-support"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Hook Support via Plugins</h3><a id="user-content-hook-support-via-plugins" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Hook Support via Plugins" href="#hook-support-via-plugins"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">OpenCode's plugin system is MORE sophisticated than Claude Code with 20+ event types:</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Claude Code Hook</th>
<th>OpenCode Plugin Event</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>PreToolUse</td>
<td><code>tool.execute.before</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PostToolUse</td>
<td><code>tool.execute.after</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Stop</td>
<td><code>session.idle</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>SessionStart</td>
<td><code>session.created</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>SessionEnd</td>
<td><code>session.deleted</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Additional OpenCode events</strong>: <code>file.edited</code>, <code>file.watcher.updated</code>, <code>message.updated</code>, <code>lsp.client.diagnostics</code>, <code>tui.toast.show</code>, and more.</p>

<p dir="auto">That npm plugin entry enables ECC's published OpenCode plugin module (hooks/events and plugin tools).
It does <strong>not</strong> automatically add ECC's full command/agent/instruction catalog to your project config.</p>
<p dir="auto">For the full ECC OpenCode setup, either:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>run OpenCode inside this repository, or</li>
<li>copy the bundled <code>.opencode/</code> config assets into your project and wire the <code>instructions</code>, <code>agent</code>, and <code>command</code> entries in <code>opencode.json</code></li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">GitHub Copilot Support</h2><a id="user-content-github-copilot-support" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: GitHub Copilot Support" href="#github-copilot-support"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Quick Start (GitHub Copilot)</h3><a id="user-content-quick-start-github-copilot" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Quick Start (GitHub Copilot)" href="#quick-start-github-copilot"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The files are already in place — open any repo that contains this project and GitHub Copilot Chat will automatically pick up <code>.github/copilot-instructions.md</code>.
The committed <code>.vscode/settings.json</code> enables <code>chat.promptFiles</code> so VS Code can load the reusable prompts from <code>.github/prompts/</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">To use the workflow prompts in Copilot Chat:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Open the Copilot Chat panel in VS Code.</li>
<li>Click the <strong>paperclip / attach</strong> icon and select <strong>Prompt...</strong>, or type <code>/</code> and choose a prompt.</li>
<li>Select the prompt (e.g. <code>plan</code>, <code>tdd</code>, <code>code-review</code>).</li>
</ol>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How It Works</h3><a id="user-content-how-it-works" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How It Works" href="#how-it-works"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">GitHub Copilot in VS Code reads two types of files automatically:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><code>.github/copilot-instructions.md</code></strong> — repository-level instructions, always injected into every Copilot Chat request. Contains ECC's core coding standards, security checklist, testing requirements, and git workflow.</li>
<li><strong><code>.github/prompts/*.prompt.md</code></strong> — reusable prompt files users invoke on demand. Each prompt walks Copilot through a specific ECC workflow (plan → TDD → review → ship).</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">The <strong><code>.vscode/settings.json</code></strong> adds per-task instruction overlays so Copilot receives the right context depending on whether you are generating code, writing tests, reviewing a selection, or drafting a commit message.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Limitations</h3><a id="user-content-limitations" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Limitations" href="#limitations"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">GitHub Copilot does not have a hook system or a subagent API, so ECC's hook automations (auto-format, TypeScript check, session persistence, dev-server guard) and agent delegation are unavailable. The instruction and prompt layer still brings the full ECC coding philosophy — standards, security, TDD, and workflow — into every Copilot Chat session.</p>
<hr>

<p dir="auto"><strong>Key architectural decisions:</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>AGENTS.md</strong> at root is the universal cross-tool file (read by Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode — GitHub Copilot uses <code>.github/copilot-instructions.md</code> instead)</li>
<li><strong>DRY adapter pattern</strong> lets Cursor reuse Claude Code's hook scripts without duplication</li>
<li><strong>Skills format</strong> (SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter) works across Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode</li>
<li>Codex's lack of hooks is compensated by <code>AGENTS.md</code>, optional <code>model_instructions_file</code> overrides, and sandbox permissions</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Background</h2><a id="user-content-background" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Background" href="#background"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">I've been using Claude Code since the experimental rollout. Won the Anthropic x Forum Ventures hackathon in Sep 2025 with <a href="https://x.com/DRodriguezFX" rel="nofollow">@DRodriguezFX</a> — built <a href="https://zenith.chat" rel="nofollow">zenith.chat</a> entirely using Claude Code.</p>
<p dir="auto">These configs are battle-tested across multiple production applications.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Token Optimization</h2><a id="user-content-token-optimization" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Token Optimization" href="#token-optimization"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Claude Code usage can be expensive if you don't manage token consumption. These settings significantly reduce costs without sacrificing quality.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Recommended Settings</h3><a id="user-content-recommended-settings" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Recommended Settings" href="#recommended-settings"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Add to <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code>:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"model"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>sonnet<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
  <span class="pl-ent">"env"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"MAX_THINKING_TOKENS"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>10000<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
    <span class="pl-ent">"CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>50<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
  }
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<tr>
<th>Setting</th>
<th>Default</th>
<th>Recommended</th>
<th>Impact</th>
</tr>

<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>model</code></td>
<td>opus</td>
<td><strong>sonnet</strong></td>
<td>~60% cost reduction; handles 80%+ of coding tasks</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>MAX_THINKING_TOKENS</code></td>
<td>31,999</td>
<td><strong>10,000</strong></td>
<td>~70% reduction in hidden thinking cost per request</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE</code></td>
<td>95</td>
<td><strong>50</strong></td>
<td>Compacts earlier — better quality in long sessions</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ECC_CONTEXT_MONITOR_COST_WARNINGS</code></td>
<td>on</td>
<td><strong>off for subscription users</strong></td>
<td>Suppresses agent-facing API-rate estimate warnings while keeping context/scope/loop warnings</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Daily Workflow Commands</h3><a id="user-content-daily-workflow-commands" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Daily Workflow Commands" href="#daily-workflow-commands"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Command</th>
<th>When to Use</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>/model sonnet</code></td>
<td>Default for most tasks</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/model opus</code></td>
<td>Complex architecture, debugging, deep reasoning</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/clear</code></td>
<td>Between unrelated tasks (free, instant reset)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/compact</code></td>
<td>At logical task breakpoints (research done, milestone complete)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/cost</code></td>
<td>Monitor token spending during session</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto">If you use a Claude subscription and the context monitor's API-rate estimates are not useful, set <code>ECC_CONTEXT_MONITOR_COST_WARNINGS=off</code>. This only suppresses the agent-facing cost warnings; it does not disable context exhaustion, scope, or loop warnings.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Strategic Compaction</h3><a id="user-content-strategic-compaction" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Strategic Compaction" href="#strategic-compaction"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The <code>strategic-compact</code> skill (included in this plugin) suggests <code>/compact</code> at logical breakpoints instead of relying on auto-compaction at 95% context. See <code>skills/strategic-compact/SKILL.md</code> for the full decision guide.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>When to compact:</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>After research/exploration, before implementation</li>
<li>After completing a milestone, before starting the next</li>
<li>After debugging, before continuing feature work</li>
<li>After a failed approach, before trying a new one</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>When NOT to compact:</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Mid-implementation (you'll lose variable names, file paths, partial state)</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Context Window Management</h3><a id="user-content-context-window-management" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Context Window Management" href="#context-window-management"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Critical:</strong> Don't enable all MCPs at once. Each MCP tool description consumes tokens from your 200k window, potentially reducing it to ~70k.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Keep under 10 MCPs enabled per project</li>
<li>Keep under 80 tools active</li>
<li>Use <code>/mcp</code> to disable unused Claude Code MCP servers; those runtime choices persist in <code>~/.claude.json</code></li>
<li>Use <code>ECC_DISABLED_MCPS</code> only to filter ECC-generated MCP configs during install/sync flows</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Agent Teams Cost Warning</h3><a id="user-content-agent-teams-cost-warning" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Agent Teams Cost Warning" href="#agent-teams-cost-warning"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Teams spawns multiple context windows. Each teammate consumes tokens independently. Only use for tasks where parallelism provides clear value (multi-module work, parallel reviews). For simple sequential tasks, subagents are more token-efficient.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">WARNING: Important Notes</h2><a id="user-content-warning-important-notes" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: WARNING: Important Notes" href="#warning-important-notes"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Token Optimization</h3><a id="user-content-token-optimization-1" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Token Optimization" href="#token-optimization-1"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Hitting daily limits? See the <strong><a href="/affaan-m/ECC/blob/main/docs/token-optimization.md">Token Optimization Guide</a></strong> for recommended settings and workflow tips.</p>
<p dir="auto">Quick wins:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-ii">// ~/.claude/settings.json</span>

  }
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Use <code>/clear</code> between unrelated tasks, <code>/compact</code> at logical breakpoints, and <code>/cost</code> to monitor spending.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Customization</h3><a id="user-content-customization" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Customization" href="#customization"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">These configs work for my workflow. You should:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Start with what resonates</li>
<li>Modify for your stack</li>
<li>Remove what you don't use</li>
